title: Prompt Injection Attacks Against LLMs
abstract: This paper explores how adversarial prompts can manipulate large language models to override safety constraints and produce unintended outputs.

title: Data Poisoning in Machine Learning Models
abstract: The study demonstrates how attackers can manipulate training datasets to alter model behavior and introduce hidden vulnerabilities.

title: Adversarial Attacks on Neural Networks
abstract: This paper presents adversarial examples that cause misclassification in deep learning models.

title: Model Extraction Attacks on ML APIs
abstract: Attackers can reconstruct machine learning models by querying APIs repeatedly and analyzing outputs.

title: Backdoor Attacks in Deep Learning
abstract: This work introduces backdoor triggers that activate malicious behavior in trained models.

title: Indirect Prompt Injection in Retrieval-Augmented Systems
abstract: This paper analyzes how malicious content embedded in external documents can manipulate LLM outputs when retrieved through RAG pipelines.

title: Membership Inference Attacks on Language Models
abstract: Attackers can determine whether specific data points were used in training large language models.

title: Model Inversion Attacks on Deep Learning Systems
abstract: This research shows how attackers reconstruct sensitive training data from model outputs.

title: Adversarial Attacks on Vision Transformers
abstract: Vision transformer models are vulnerable to adversarial perturbations that degrade classification accuracy.

title: Data Poisoning Attacks in Federated Learning
abstract: Malicious participants inject poisoned updates to manipulate the global model.

title: Backdoor Attacks via Training Data Manipulation
abstract: Backdoor triggers are inserted into training data to activate malicious behaviors.

title: Extraction of Language Models via API Queries
abstract: Repeated API queries allow attackers to approximate the internal behavior of language models.

title: Adversarial Prompting Attacks on Chatbots
abstract: Adversarial prompts can force chatbots to generate unintended or unsafe responses.

title: Security Risks in Large Language Models
abstract: This paper surveys vulnerabilities in LLMs including prompt injection and data leakage.

title: Privacy Leakage in Language Models
abstract: Language models may unintentionally leak sensitive information from training data.

title: Adversarial Examples in NLP Models
abstract: Text-based adversarial examples can mislead NLP systems into incorrect outputs.

title: RAG-Based System Vulnerabilities
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems are vulnerable to malicious document injection.

title: Model Stealing Attacks on AI Systems
abstract: Attackers replicate models by analyzing outputs and predictions.

title: Security Challenges in AI Deployment
abstract: Discusses risks when deploying AI systems including exploitation and misuse.

title: Chain-of-Thought Prompt Leakage
abstract: Attackers exploit model reasoning outputs to extract intermediate steps and sensitive information.

title: Jailbreak Attacks on Conversational AI
abstract: This paper demonstrates techniques to bypass safety filters in conversational AI systems.

title: Adversarial Attacks on Speech Recognition Systems
abstract: Hidden perturbations in audio inputs can mislead speech-to-text systems into incorrect outputs.

title: Trojan Attacks in Neural Networks
abstract: Neural networks can be embedded with hidden triggers that activate malicious functionality.

title: Model Stealing via Prediction APIs
abstract: Attackers can reconstruct proprietary models by analyzing prediction outputs over time.

title: Data Leakage in Fine-Tuned Language Models
abstract: Fine-tuned models may memorize and leak sensitive training data.

title: Adversarial Attacks on Recommendation Systems
abstract: Attackers manipulate recommendation outputs by injecting malicious user behavior data.

title: Poisoning Attacks in Reinforcement Learning
abstract: Adversaries manipulate reward signals to alter agent behavior in reinforcement learning.

title: Privacy Attacks on Generative Models
abstract: Generative models may leak sensitive information through generated outputs.

title: Security Risks in Autonomous AI Agents
abstract: Autonomous AI agents may execute unintended or malicious actions due to manipulation or misalignment.

title: Adversarial Attacks on Graph Neural Networks
abstract: Attackers manipulate graph structures to mislead graph neural network predictions.

title: Poisoning Attacks on Recommendation Engines
abstract: Malicious user interactions influence recommendation outputs.

title: Model Evasion Attacks in NLP Systems
abstract: Carefully crafted text inputs bypass NLP detection systems.

title: Adversarial Attacks on Object Detection Models
abstract: Visual perturbations can cause incorrect object detection in images.

title: Federated Learning Model Poisoning
abstract: Participants inject malicious updates to compromise federated models.

title: Membership Inference on Generative Models
abstract: Attackers infer whether specific data exists in generative model training sets.

title: Neural Network Trojan Attacks
abstract: Hidden triggers cause neural networks to behave maliciously.

title: Data Extraction from Chatbots
abstract: Chatbots may reveal confidential data through repeated queries.

title: Backdoor Attacks on Image Classifiers
abstract: Triggers embedded in images activate malicious model responses.

title: Adversarial Attacks on Time Series Models
abstract: Perturbations in sequential data mislead predictive models.

title: Prompt Injection in Multi-Agent Systems
abstract: Attackers manipulate interactions between multiple AI agents.

title: Data Leakage via Model Outputs
abstract: Models unintentionally expose sensitive data through predictions.

title: Adversarial Attacks on Reinforcement Learning Agents
abstract: Adversaries manipulate environment signals to alter agent decisions.

title: Model Extraction from Black-Box Systems
abstract: Black-box access allows attackers to approximate model behavior.

title: Adversarial Attacks on GANs
abstract: Generative models can be manipulated to produce malicious outputs.

title: Prompt Injection in Code Generation Models
abstract: Attackers manipulate code generation outputs through malicious prompts.

title: Security Risks in AI-Based Decision Systems
abstract: AI decision systems can be manipulated to produce harmful outcomes.

title: Adversarial Attacks on Multimodal Models
abstract: Combining image and text perturbations to mislead multimodal AI systems.

title: Data Poisoning in Autonomous Driving Systems
abstract: Training data manipulation can affect decision-making in autonomous vehicles.

title: AI Model Misalignment Exploitation
abstract: Misaligned AI systems can be manipulated to produce unintended harmful behaviors.
